rows.question: What is the Bachelor of Liberal Studies (BLS) degree?

single.answer:
The Bachelor of Liberal Studies (BLS) at the University of Illinois is an online, asynchronous degree program designed for students seeking a flexible, interdisciplinary education. The curriculum emphasizes focused, broad-based learning that allows you to engage with a variety of academic perspectives while working toward your degree.

Key details about the program include:

*   **Format:** Courses are delivered online and asynchronously, providing flexibility for your schedule while maintaining the structure of standard academic terms.
*   **Curriculum:** The program offers a multidisciplinary approach. You can find information regarding current concentrations and course offerings on the [program page](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/).
*   **Degree Requirements:** The program requires the completion of specific credit hours to graduate. 
*   **Transfer Credit:** The university accepts transfer credit toward the degree when it is supported by official transcripts and meets program requirements.
*   **Admissions:** For the most current information regarding deadlines, requirements, and key dates, please consult the [Admissions FAQ](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/admissions/#faq).

If you have specific questions about the application process or your individual academic standing, please contact the Office of Undergraduate Admissions or reach out to our team at onlineBLS@illinois.edu.

dual.answer:
The Bachelor of Liberal Studies (BLS) is an online, asynchronous degree program offered by the University of Illinois. It is designed to provide students with a focused, interdisciplinary learning experience that allows for flexibility in coursework.

Key features of the program include:

*   **Structure:** The program is delivered entirely online and follows an asynchronous format, allowing students to engage with course materials on their own schedule within the bounds of the semester.
*   **Curriculum:** The BLS curriculum emphasizes interdisciplinary study. You can find detailed information regarding current program concentrations and course offerings on the [program page](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/).
*   **Degree Requirements:** The degree is comprised of a specific number of credit hours. For details regarding admission requirements, prerequisites, and transfer credit policies, please consult the [Admissions FAQ](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/admissions/#faq).
*   **Admissions Support:** For questions regarding your specific academic background, the application process, or enrollment, please contact the Office of Undergraduate Admissions or email onlineBLS@illinois.edu for further guidance.

rows.question: Can I transfer credits? How many?

single.answer:
Yes, the Bachelor of Liberal Studies (BLS) program accepts transfer credits from accredited institutions. 

Regarding your specific transfer questions:

*   **Evaluation:** Transfer credit is accepted when supported by official transcripts and evaluated for applicability to the degree requirements.
*   **Credit Hour Requirements:** The program requires a minimum of 120 credit hours to graduate. You can review the structure of the degree and requirements for the major on the [program page](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/).
*   **Admissions Process:** Specific details regarding how credits are applied and the transfer admissions process are available in the [Admissions FAQ](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/admissions/#faq).

For personalized guidance regarding your academic history or the transfer application process, please contact the Office of Undergraduate Admissions or reach out to our team at onlineBLS@illinois.edu.

dual.answer:
Thank you for your interest in the Bachelor of Liberal Studies (BLS) program.

There is no minimum GPA requirement for admission to the BLS program. To be considered for admission, applicants must have completed at least 30 transferable credit hours from an accredited institution.

For further details regarding admission requirements and the application process, please review the [Admissions FAQ](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/admissions/#faq). rows.question: Are there required courses?

single.answer:
The Bachelor of Liberal Studies (BLS) program is designed to provide a focused, interdisciplinary learning experience. 

Regarding required courses:

*   The BLS curriculum consists of 120 credit hours in total.
*   The program is delivered in an online and asynchronous format.
*   Because requirements can vary based on individual academic history and transfer credit, please review the specific course structures and curriculum details on the [program page](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/).
*   If you have questions regarding your specific academic path or how your previous coursework may apply, please contact the Office of Undergraduate Admissions or reach out to us at onlineBLS@illinois.edu.

For the most current information regarding degree requirements and general policies, you may also consult the [Admissions FAQ](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/admissions/#faq).

rows.question: How long does it take to complete the degree?

single.answer:
The Bachelor of Liberal Studies (BLS) program is designed as an online and asynchronous degree, allowing you to engage with course materials on a schedule that fits your commitments.

Because the time required to complete the degree depends on your individual academic background, such as the number of transfer credit hours you bring into the program, there is no set timeframe for completion. 

To help you plan your path to graduation, please consider the following:

*   **Degree Requirements:** The program requires the completion of 120 total credit hours.
*   **Transfer Credit:** The program accepts transfer credit from accredited institutions; once you apply, the Office of Undergraduate Admissions will review your previous coursework to determine how it applies toward your degree.
*   **Program Details:** You can find more information about the curriculum and current offerings on the [program page](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/).
*   **Admissions Guidance:** For specific questions regarding your academic history or the application process, please consult the [Admissions FAQ](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/admissions/#faq).

If you have additional questions regarding your specific situation, please reach out to the program team at onlineBLS@illinois.edu.

rows.question: Is BLS a good option if I'm returning to school after many years?

single.answer:
The Bachelor of Liberal Studies (BLS) is designed for students who are ready to complete their degree through an online, asynchronous format. This program offers a focused, interdisciplinary approach to learning, allowing you to integrate diverse academic perspectives into your education.

If you are considering returning to school, please keep the following in mind:

*   **Program Structure:** The BLS is delivered entirely online and is asynchronous, meaning you engage with course materials on your own schedule while meeting course deadlines.
*   **Transfer Credit:** We accept transfer credit toward the degree when supported by your official transcripts. 
*   **Curriculum:** The program emphasizes interdisciplinary study. You can view current concentration options and academic requirements on our [program page](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/).
*   **Admissions Process:** For specific questions regarding your application or the enrollment process, please review our [Admissions FAQ](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/admissions/#faq).

If you have additional questions regarding your specific academic background, please contact the Office of Undergraduate Admissions or email us directly at onlineBLS@illinois.edu.

The Bachelor of Liberal Studies (BLS) program is designed to accommodate students who are balancing professional and personal commitments. 

Key details regarding the structure of the program include:

*   **100% Online Delivery:** The entire curriculum is offered online, providing the flexibility to study from any location.
*   **Asynchronous Format:** Courses are delivered asynchronously. This means that while you will still meet assignment deadlines, you are not required to log in at specific times for live lectures, allowing you to engage with course materials on a schedule that fits your needs.
*   **Program Planning:** Information regarding enrollment status, such as credit hour requirements for part-time or full-time designation, can be found in our [Admissions FAQ](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/admissions/#faq).

rows.question: Can BLS help me pivot industries?

single.answer:
The Bachelor of Liberal Studies (BLS) program at the University of Illinois is designed to provide focused, interdisciplinary learning that can help you strengthen your professional profile. By developing essential skills in communication, critical thinking, and complex problem-solving, the program allows you to apply a broad academic background to various career fields.

Whether you are seeking to transition into a new industry or advance in your current one, the BLS program provides a structured way to build upon your prior educational experience.

*   **Curriculum:** The program is entirely online and asynchronous, offering the flexibility to earn your degree while managing other commitments.
*   **Academic Planning:** You can explore the specific structure of the degree and current concentrations on the [program page](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/).
*   **Transfer Credits:** The university accepts transfer credit when supported by official documentation; please consult the [Admissions FAQ](https://lasonline.illinois.edu/programs/bls/admissions/#faq) for guidance on the admissions process.

If you have specific questions regarding how your previous coursework aligns with the degree requirements, please contact the Office of Undergraduate Admissions or reach out to us at onlineBLS@illinois.edu for further assistance.
